chunk_summary: Guidelines for weight-based F-75 prescription in SAM focus on metabolic stabilization, precise feed volumes, and progression rules to prevent complications like hypoglycemia and vomiting.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of F-75 in the stabilization phase of SAM management.
Learn how to calculate and prescribe weight-based F-75 feed volumes.
Recognize triggers for NG feeding and reassessment in cases of poor intake.
Apply feeding progression rules to ensure safe therapeutic feeding.
key_facts: F-75 is a low-sodium, low-protein therapeutic feed used for metabolic stabilization in SAM.
Initial feeding targets are approximately 130 mL/kg/day, reduced to 100 mL/kg/day in severe edema (+++).
Feeds are given every 2 hours initially, transitioning to 3-4 hours as the child stabilizes.
Minimum acceptable intake is 80% of the daily target; NG top-up is initiated if intake falls below this threshold.
Vomiting management includes reducing feed volume and increasing frequency rather than skipping feeds.
Breastfeeding should continue on demand alongside therapeutic feeds.
reference_quote: Use low-sodium, low-protein F-75 (or equivalent starter diet) given day and night in small, frequent feeds. Write orders in executable bedside terms: 'F-75 ___ mL per feed every ___ hours; daily target ___ mL; minimum acceptable intake (80% of target) ___ mL/day.'
document_key_topics: Management of hypoglycemia and vomiting in SAM
Feeding progression and NG feeding protocols
